source: Incus Server - 1 Install and Configuration: Environment setup: limits.conf and the sysctl override

questions.q: Which file sets the open-file and memlock limits for Incus?
questions.why: limits.conf carries the nofile and memlock soft/hard limits.

questions.q: Raising nofile in limits.conf prevents which error under many containers?
questions.why: Too many containers exhaust the default open-file limit; raising nofile avoids it.

questions.q: Where do the kernel (sysctl) overrides for Incus go?
Why use a drop-in file in /etc/sysctl.d rather than editing the main config?
options: systemd lets you override the specific settings you need without touching the main configuration file
questions.why: A drop-in overrides just the needed settings, leaving the main file alone.

questions.q: After editing limits.conf and the sysctl override, what is needed?
options: Only log out and back in
questions.why: A reboot applies the new limits and kernel parameters.

questions.q: Check the running value of the kernel parameter net.core.bpf_jit_limit.
questions.why: sysctl <key> prints the current value, to confirm the override took effect.

questions.q: Which kinds of settings does the override raise for running many containers?
questions.why: It raises inotify queue/instances/watches, vm.max_map_count, ARP thresholds, bpf_jit_limit, keys, aio, etc.
